title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: The history of music is (0) inextricably linked to the development of the tools used to create it. Archaeologists have discovered flutes made from bird bone that (1) back over forty thousand years, suggesting that music has always been a fundamental part of human life. In ancient civilizations, instruments were often (2) with religious rituals and social ceremonies. Over the centuries, the design of these instruments became increasingly (3) as craftsmen experimented with new materials and techniques. The transition from wooden to metal components, for instance, allowed for a greater (4) of sound and durability. By the time of the Renaissance, instrument making had become a highly (5) craft, with certain families gaining international fame for their violins and lutes. These makers were known for their (6) attention to detail, ensuring that every curve and string produced the perfect tone. The (7) of the piano in the eighteenth century further revolutionized the musical landscape, providing composers with a versatile tool for expression. Even today, despite the rise of digital technology, acoustic instruments continue to (8) a unique charm. They remain essential for musicians who seek a physical connection to their art, proving that the traditional craftsmanship of the past still has a significant place in the modern, high-tech world of music.

example.explanation: “Inextricably linked” is a common collocation meaning two things are impossible to separate.

questions.explanation: “Date back” is the standard phrasal verb used for historical age.
“Associated with” is the correct collocation for connecting two ideas or things.
“Sophisticated” is used here to describe complex and advanced designs.
“Range of sound” is the standard musical term for the variety of notes or tones.
“Specialized craft” describes a job requiring specific and high-level skills.
“Meticulous attention to detail” is a very common fixed collocation.
“The advent of” is a formal term meaning the arrival or invention of something important.
“Hold a charm” is an idiomatic way to say something remains attractive or special.
